issue_title: rg panic caused by --replace, --multiline, a particular pattern, and search text containing repeats and newlines
issue_body:
When using the --replace and --multiline flags with certain search patterns, ripgrep panics when it searches text containing certain sequences of repeated text and newlines. The panic message is "slice index starts at x but ends at y", where x and y are integers and y < x.

Each minimal search pattern is extremely specific. Removing almost any element will prevent the bug from appearing.

Reproduction test case 1:
```
lines2=$(printf " b b b b b b b b\nc")
echo "$lines2" > lines2.txt
rg "(^|[^a-z])((([a-z]+)?)\s)?b(\s([a-z]+)?)($|[^a-z])" --replace=x --multiline lines2.txt
```

Reproduction test case 2:
```
lines5=$(printf " b\nb\nb\nb\nc")
echo "$lines5" > lines5.txt
rg "(^|[^a-z])((([a-z]+)?)\s)?b(\s([a-z]+)?)($|[^a-z])" --replace=x --multiline lines5.txt
```

Actual behavior: ripgrep panics with backtrace:
```
thread 'main' panicked at crates/printer/src/util.rs:568:22:
slice index starts at 18 but ends at 17
```
The panic occurs in `grep_printer::util::replace_with_captures_in_context` because the invariant that the match would never exceed the range given is not always true when look-around is involved.

Expected behavior: ripgrep should return matching text with replacements applied, without panicking.
